title: Fancy Closet Storage Tower
summary: A freestanding plywood closet tower with a face frame, decorative header, and matching mitered baseboard — a floor-standing cabinet, unlike this catalog's wall-hung hanging closet system.
description:
This tower stands on its own base rather than hanging from a wall cleat, and it's dressed up with a header, base trim, and edge-banded shelf edges rather than left as a plain plywood box. Build the basic carcass with pocket holes on the outsides — top and bottom of the top and bottom panels — then decide between fixed shelves at any height, or adjustable shelves on shelf pins, which need trimming down about 1/4" in width and depth to clear the pins and allow easy placement.

**If you go with adjustable shelves, the tower needs a back panel** — without fixed shelves tying the sides together, nothing else stops the carcass from splaying out in the middle. Build the face frame as its own flat assembly, then attach it to the front with glue and finish nails, which also finishes off every exposed plywood edge on the front.

The base gets trimmed out in baseboard matching the room's existing trim, mitered at the corners, and the optional decorative header — cut with a jigsaw for a shaped detail plus a small inside-corner moulding — is purely cosmetic, not structural.

materials.name: 3/4" plywood, ripped to 15-3/4" strips, full sheet
Inside corner moulding, 3/4" x 3/4" (optional header trim)
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.material: 3/4" plywood, ripped to 15-3/4" strips, full sheet
3/4" plywood, ripped to 15-3/4" strips, full sheet
cutList.note: Trim 1/4" in width and depth if using adjustable shelf pins.
Cut to the height of your baseboard.
Cut to the tower's outside depth minus the baseboard depth.
Cut to fit, mitered at corners.

steps.title: Build the carcass
steps.body: Build the basic box with 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws, placing pocket holes on the outsides.
materials: 3/4" plywood, ripped to 15-3/4" strips, full sheet

steps.title: Set the shelves
steps.body: For adjustable shelves, drill shelf pin holes with a shelf pin jig and trim the shelves down to clear the pins. For fixed shelves, place at any height and finish the front plywood edges with wood filler or edge banding.
materials: 3/4" plywood, ripped to 15-3/4" strips, full sheet

steps.title: Add the back
steps.body: If using adjustable shelves, attach the back panel with nails and glue to keep the tower from splaying.

steps.title: Build and attach the face frame
steps.body: Build the face frame with pocket holes, then attach to the front with glue and finish nails.

steps.title: Add the base and header
steps.body: Build the optional base from the legs and rails, then trim it in mitered baseboard matching the room. Add the optional header trim and corner moulding, cutting the header detail with a jigsaw.
materials: Inside corner moulding, 3/4" x 3/4" (optional header trim)

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

images.alt: Fancy closet storage tower with face frame and header
